Stefan is transported to Scripps Mercy Hospital, where he's rushed into surgery. According to police documents, Officer Robert Gladis fired his weapon approximately nine times, six rounds striking Stefan directly, while two bullet holes were found on the side of the nearby apartment. At the hospital, Stefan undergoes emergency surgery for multiple gunshot wounds, including a fractured humerus and a bullet that clipped the artery in his right thigh, ultimately surviving the incident. Toxicology reports reveal a blood alcohol level of 0.130, along with fentanyl, THC, opiates, and methamphetamine in his system. At the time of his arrest, Stefan was not carrying a weapon. However, officers later recovered a pistol he had dropped in the parking lot while fleeing, one that an officer unknowingly ran right past during the pursuit. What the officer believed to be a weapon in Stefan's hand at the moment he opened fire, was actually a cell phone. As for the baby, she was evaluated by medics on scene and found to have no major injuries before being returned safely to her mother. According to news sources, in March 2025, Stefan Nuttle pleaded guilty to multiple charges, including child endangerment and assault with a semi-automatic firearm. On May 1st, 2025, he was sentenced to 14 years and eight months in state prison. Following his sentencing, Stefan filed a federal lawsuit against Officer Robert Gladis, alleging the use of excessive and unjustified force during the incident. As of March 21st, 2025, the case remains pending in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of California, with a joint status report filed by Stefan's legal team.
